channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: This agency is tasked with protecting elections from cyber attacks. If Trump wins, it could be in danger
item.description: Jen Easterly’s confirmation by the Senate in July 2021 to become director of the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) was about as smooth as Senate votes get. With a résumé that includes time in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Max Ufberg, Fast Company

item.title: Nigeria releases American crypto executive after dropping money laundering case
item.description: An American cryptocurrency executive held in Nigeria for the past eight months has been released after authorities there announced that they were ending his money laundering trial on health and diplomatic grounds.
item.dc:creator: Chinedu Asadu And Eric Tucker, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: AI is now a crucial tool in Trump’s image-management arsenal
item.description: An image of Donald Trump cosplaying as a football player last weekend set the tone for the final push of the Republican nominee’s campaign. It depicts Trump as a yoked, veiny-arm baller glowering from within a Steelers uniform on the field at Pittsburgh’s Acrisure Stadium.
item.dc:creator: Joe Berkowitz, Fast Company

item.title: The Secret Codes to Unlocking Netflix's Hidden Gems: A Guide to Navigating the Streaming Giant's Enormous Library
item.description: Discover the secret codes to unlocking Netflix's hidden gems, from horror movies to niche categories and everything in between. Learn how to use these shortcuts to fast-track your search and find the best titles on the platform.
item.dc:creator: lucy.buglass@futurenet.com (Lucy Buglass), TechRadar

item.title: SEC Fines Four Companies Over Misleading Cybersecurity Disclosures
item.description: Four companies slapped with fines for downplaying cybersecurity breaches, US officials urge Trump's campaign to prepare for transition, and more
item.dc:creator: Eric White, Federal News Network

item.title: One Tech Tip: How to prepare your online accounts for when you die
item.description: Most people have accumulated a pile of data on their social media and digital accounts over their lifetimes.
item.dc:creator: Kelvin Chan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: 'Wicked' star Ariana Grande's gripe with AI
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Network jobs watch: Hiring, skills and certification trends
item.description: Network and infrastructure roles continue to shift as enterprises adopt technologies such as AI-driven network operations, multicloud networking, zero trust network access (ZTNA), and SD-WAN.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Hydrogen startup Verdagy opens San Jose plant at risky moment
item.description: The Silicon Valley company is firing up its electrolyzer factory even as hydrogen demand has been slow to grow.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Deception in democracy: Beware the most common types of election-related scams
item.description: Cybersecurity risks are ramping up as the Nov. 5 U.S. general election approaches. Social media and the internet are used to spread propaganda.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: As coal plants shut in Romania, some miners transition to green energy while others are reluctant
item.description: PETRILA, Romania (AP) — For many years, Sebastian Tirinticǎ worked in a coal mine, just like his father and grandfather before him.
item.dc:creator: ANCA GURZU, Cipher News, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Commercial space companies now able to churn out satellites
item.description: Increased satellite production is now becoming the standard now in the space hardware industry. The post Commercial space companies now able to churn out satellites first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Eric White, Federal News Network

item.title: Big offshore wind project proposed for New York as other sites are evaluated in 3 states
item.description: BRIGANTINE, N.J. (AP) — Offshore wind energy projects in New York, New Jersey and Maryland are moving forward, as federal regulators examine the proposals and opponents escalate their legal challenges to the work.
item.dc:creator: WAYNE PARRY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Elon Musk says he’ll fix the government under Trump. His track record paints a different picture
item.description: Elon Musk is making some big promises while campaigning for former President Donald Trump. But his business record calls into question whether he can deliver.
item.dc:creator: CNN.com, The Mercury News

item.title: cPacket doubles the speed of its packet capture appliance
item.description: cPacket Networks this week launched a new appliance designed to capture network traffic and enable network operations teams to achieve better efficiency by monitoring network reliability in real time while also looking backward to investigate prior incidents.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Don’t panic. Here’s how to find your photos after iOS 18 update
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson has the details on the iOS 18 Photos app: Revamped layout, customizable collections and easier photo organization.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: FTC's rule banning fake online reviews goes into effect
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal rule banning fake online reviews is now in effect. The Federal Trade Commission issued the rule in August banning the sale or purchase of online reviews.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Rust 1.82 brings Cargo info subcommand
item.description: Rust 1.82, the newest version of the popular memory-safe programming language, fulfills a longstanding request for displaying information in the Cargo package manager registry. Also with Rust 1.82, Apple macOS on 64-bit Arm now offers Rust’s highest guarantee of functionality.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: One of the world’s largest book publishers adds AI warnings to its books
item.description: To make generative AI tools (genAI) work as well as possible, tech companies have chosen to train their large language models (LLMs) on large amounts of text, even though doing so could run afoul of copyright laws.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Biden administration to provide $325 million for new Michigan semiconductor factory
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration said Monday that it would provide up to $325 million to Hemlock Semiconductor for a new factory, a move that could help give Democrats a political edge in the swing state of Michigan ahead of election day.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Recycling trucks with AI-powered cameras raise privacy concerns
item.description: The artificial intelligence can detect if there are any improperly recycled items and will log the information accordingly.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: October is Cybersecurity Awareness Month. Here's how to stay safe from scams
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — October is Cybersecurity Awareness Month, which means it's the perfect time to learn how to protect yourself from scams. “Scams have become so sophisticated now.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA MORGA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US to probe Tesla's 'Full Self-Driving' system after pedestrian killed in low visibility conditions
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — The U.S. government's road safety agency is investigating Tesla's “Full Self-Driving” system after getting reports of crashes in low-visibility conditions, including one that killed a pedestrian.
item.dc:creator: TOM KRISHER, Associated Press, KRON4
